title: Seed permission-key → live rbac.config (resource:action) taxonomy map
description: Bridges the 3-segment field-level seed/registry keys (module:resource:action, authored per ADR-035/seed) to the 2-segment live rbac.config.js capability model (resource:action). This is the MISSING input a resolver-collapse needs (AUTHZ_MODERNIZATION_PLAN §5.1): the seed is a strict MANY-TO-ONE REFINEMENT of rbac.config (e.g. beneficiary:demographics:read + beneficiary:clinical:read both → beneficiaries:read), so can() and the legacy hasPermission() cannot be compared key-for-key without this map. PURE DATA — additive, nothing consumes it yet; it is the analog of role-archetype.map.json (roles) for permission KEYS. 'rbac' = { resource, action } in rbac.config; null = no 2-segment equivalent (the refinement is finer than the live model can express — these are the field-level distinctions the live model is BLIND to, the main reason the new matrix is more granular). 'note' records refinement collisions + intent.

map.note: NO live equivalent — rbac.config has no 'branches' resource (branch CRUD is not a capability in the 2-segment model). Candidate new resource on collapse.
no rbac 'branches' resource
no rbac 'branches' resource
no rbac 'branches' resource
branch-scoped settings → settings:update (no branch-settings resource in rbac)
REFINEMENT: collapses with beneficiary:clinical:read onto beneficiaries:read — rbac is blind to the demographics/clinical field split
REFINEMENT: PHI-gated read; rbac cannot distinguish from demographics:read
REFINEMENT (W1509): clinical context shown in the WhatsApp Inbox sidebar — collapses with beneficiary:clinical:read onto beneficiaries:read
REFINEMENT (W1509): clinical internal notes on a WhatsApp conversation — rbac (2-seg) collapses onto beneficiaries:read; can() (3-seg) gates write/read precisely
seed 'billing' = live 'invoices' (also 'finance' exists — invoices is the per-beneficiary billing resource)
soft-delete/deactivate → beneficiaries:delete (rbac has no deactivate action)
seed 'treatment_plan' = live 'care_plans' (underscore; care_plans_ddd is the V4 twin)
supersede (versioning) → care_plans:update (no supersede action in rbac)
REFINEMENT: collapses with session:schedule:read onto sessions:read
seed 'appointment' = live 'schedules' (no 'appointments' resource in rbac)
REFINEMENT: hr:leave / hr:payroll / hr:performance all collapse onto the single 'hr' resource — rbac is blind to the HR sub-domain split
payroll has its OWN rbac resource (unlike leave/performance) — finer than the generic hr resource
process → payroll:update (no process action in rbac)
REFINEMENT: all 5 report:* sub-domains collapse onto reports:read/export — rbac is blind to operational/clinical/financial/hr/audit report split (the field-level confidentiality the new matrix adds)
no dedicated 'approvals' resource in rbac; approval workflow rides on the 'workflow' resource (closest live equivalent)
disable = soft-delete → users:delete
role grant/revoke is a user-administration op → users:manage (rbac has no 'roles' resource)
NO live equivalent — rbac.config has no 'permissions'/'rbac' resource; policy management is a genuine gap in the 2-segment model. Candidate new resource on collapse.
audit logs are append-only by design (D9); update/delete exist as keys only to be explicitly DENIED to everyone in the seed
append-only — deny-to-all permission

rules.coverage: every key in permissions.registry.js ALL[] MUST appear exactly once in map[].key; a new seed permission with no taxonomy mapping fails the W669 drift guard
rules.targetIntegrity: every map[].rbac.resource MUST be a value in rbac.config.RESOURCES and every map[].rbac.action a value in rbac.config.ACTIONS (null allowed for keys with no 2-segment equivalent — currently none)
rules.refinement: the seed→rbac direction is MANY-TO-ONE: multiple field-level seed keys legitimately map to the same rbac (resource,action). This is expected and is the whole reason the new matrix is more granular. The REVERSE (rbac→seed) is therefore one-to-many and is NOT a function — a resolver-collapse must keep can() (3-seg) as the authority and treat rbac as the coarse legacy view.
rules.doNotCollapseYet: this map ENABLES a future resolver-collapse but does not perform it; wiring can() into the live gates is owner-gated (ADR-037).
